message:
refactor(core): replace low-value trait tests with behavioral assertions across 33 modules

Systematically upgrade test quality across the core module suite by
replacing auto-derived trait verification tests (Debug/Clone/Copy/Eq)
with meaningful behavioral assertions that test actual module semantics.

This affects 33 source files spanning:
- cancel/symbol_cancel: verify cancellation propagation semantics
- channel/{mpsc,oneshot,session}: test backpressure, ordering, close
- combinator/{bulkhead,hedge,retry}: verify policy enforcement under load
- cx/{macaroon,scope}: test context isolation and capability attenuation
- distributed/{bridge,consistent_hash,recovery,snapshot}: verify
  partition tolerance and consistency guarantees
- grpc/codec, http/h1/{codec,stream}: test frame boundary handling
- io/copy: verify zero-copy passthrough correctness
- net/quic_core: test connection state machine transitions
- record/{region,symbol_obligation_tracker}: verify obligation tracking
- sync/{pool,semaphore}: test fairness and starvation prevention
- tls/{acceptor,connector,stream,types}: verify handshake state machine
- transport/router: test routing table consistency
- types/budget, web/debug, session, plan/fixtures: misc improvements

Each replacement follows the same pattern: remove tests that only assert
compiler-generated trait impls, add tests that exercise the module's
actual contract and failure modes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
